carbon_pricing.power_sector_notes: Power generation classified as non-EITE (domestic-oriented). Full carbon tax liability applies with no transitional allowances. Costs pass through to consumers via electricity tariffs.
carbon_pricing.source: National Climate Change Secretariat (NCCS), Carbon Pricing Act 2018, Budget 2022

solar_pv.notes: Singapore solar LCOE 2-3x higher than regional benchmarks due to severe land constraints (709 km² total area). Large-scale C&I rooftop ~$63/MWh, small-scale residential ~$110/MWh. Current installed capacity: 1,211 MWac (2.1% of electricity).
solar_pv.source: EMA Singapore Energy Statistics 2024, SERIS, Solar Magazine

solar_plus_storage.notes: 4-hour battery storage alone ~$104/MWh (BNEF 2024). Competitive APAC solar+storage hybrids $60-80/MWh. Cross-border projects (Indonesia, Australia) will leverage regional cost advantages.

wind_onshore.notes: Average wind speeds 2-3 m/s, well below 4-5 m/s commercial threshold. Land scarcity and maritime traffic restrict options. Only installation: 20 kW demonstration turbine at Semakau Landfill.

wind_offshore.notes: Maritime traffic density and limited territorial waters constrain offshore wind. No commercial projects planned.

gas_ccgt.notes: Natural gas provides ~94% of Singapore electricity. Modern CCGT plants achieve 52-60% efficiency. Grid emission factor: 0.402 kg CO2/kWh (2024). Wholesale electricity price (USEP) ranges $75-150/MWh.
gas_ccgt.source: EMA Singapore Energy Statistics 2024, Ember ASEAN Clean Power Pathways 2024

coal.notes: Coal is not competitive in Singapore due to import costs, carbon tax exposure, and policy direction. Only Tembusu Multi-Utilities Complex uses coal/gas/biomass mix (<2% of generation). New coal effectively banned.
coal.source: IRENA Renewable Power Generation Costs 2024, Lazard LCOE+ v17.0

national_targets.re_target_notes: 2 GWp solar target would power ~350,000 households. Current installed: 1.17-1.21 GW (60% of 2030 goal). Interim target: 1.5 GWp by 2025.
national_targets.coal_phaseout_notes: Phase out of unabated coal power by 2050. Joined Powering Past Coal Alliance (PPCA) at COP26 (Nov 2021). Current coal dependency <2% (only Tembusu Multi-Utilities Complex uses coal/gas/biomass mix).
national_targets.net_zero_notes: Announced October 25, 2022 at Singapore International Energy Week. Power sector has dedicated 2050 net-zero target. Public sector target: ~2045.

electricity_import_target.notes: Raised from 4 GW to 6 GW in September 2024. 11 projects with conditional approval spanning Australia, Cambodia, Indonesia, Vietnam, Malaysia (Sarawak).

national_targets.source: Singapore Green Plan 2030, NCCS Long-Term Low-Emissions Development Strategy (LEDS), Energy Market Authority, MSE press releases, 2035 NDC submission (Feb 2025)

power_sector_context.notes: ~95% of gas imported via pipeline (Malaysia/Indonesia) and LNG terminals. Land-constrained city-state with 709 km² total area. System reserve margin ~30%.
